title: Volvo and Polestar EV Strategy Leading the Race
text: Polestar evolved from Volvo's racing skunkworks into standalone EV offshoot jointly owned by Volvo and Chinese mothership Geely. Volvo acquired Polestar in July 2015, repositioning brand as EV manufacturer since 2017. While related, brands compete in different segments. Volvo remains full-line premium automaker offering mild hybrids, plug-in hybrids and fully electric models. Polestar positions as EV-first brand focused on performance, design experimentation and minimalist tech-forward vibe. Volvo appeals to customers wanting premium vehicle with broader powertrains and comfort-first feel tied to safety. In February 2026, Polestar announced four new EVs planned for market by end of 2028. Swedish brand majority-owned by Geely and affiliated with Volvo enters expansion on back of strongest retail sales year. Full-year 2025 retail sales reached approximately 60,119 cars, 34% increase versus 2024. Strategic positioning allows Polestar to pursue performance-oriented EV brand while leveraging Volvo and Geely manufacturing, technology and capital.
